rationale:
Codehab is Next.js 15 + Supabase + Anthropic SDK with 10+ routes (admin, auth, join, members, my-project, room, schedule, submit) across 26 commits — the highest commit count of any submission with public source. Real auth flow, real DB-backed project lifecycle, calendar integration, and an explicit @anthropic-ai/sdk dependency suggests an LLM-powered feedback or summarization feature is wired into the project flow. Components (Navbar, ProjectCard, ProjectModal, CalendarSidebar, EmailPrompt, MemberProfileModal) match a multi-page community PM tool, not a single dashboard. The weekStatus() helper tracks active vs. past vs. future weeks with voting deadlines — domain-aware, not generic.

The pitch ('80 strangers into a tight-knit builder community, every project gets a stage, find collaborators, schedule tasks on your calendar') aligns directly with Roger's mid-week 'PM tools are a motivation layer' reframe, and the code reflects that framing: schedule + collab + project staging rather than a generic Kanban. Equal to jfleck25 (Momentum) in real-backend depth and arguably broader in product surface (route count), but slightly less domain-specific in schema design (no equivalent of Momentum's streak / ship-event modeling). Both are 9s for fundamentally different reasons — Momentum is the deepest single-axis backend, Codehab is the widest multi-page real product. Held back from 10 by the same auth-wall ceiling as Momentum: schema + dependencies confirm real work, but the actual user-facing motivation mechanics behind the login can't be verified from code alone.
